note: Also see Exhibit D-1 State Addenda to Franchise Agreement (pp 183-196) which was not deeply transcribed in this run.

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor's liquidated damages formula may not hold in CA; exposure capped or litigated.

overrides.override_summary: CA Bus & Prof Code §§20000-20043 govern termination and non-renewal rights
NY choice-of-law may not be enforceable under CA law
Fairfax County VA venue may not be enforceable under CA law
CA Corp Code §31512 voids waivers under CA Franchise Investment Law; Bus & Prof Code §20010 voids waivers under Franchise Relations Act
CA Corp Code §31125 requires disclosure document before material modification
General release does not relieve franchisor from HI franchise-law liability
Item 13: franchisor does not own Marks — affiliate owns them and licenses/sublicenses
Termination and non-renewal rights affected by IL law 815 ILCS 705/19 and 705/20
Governing law, jurisdiction, venue, choice of law, and jury waiver do not constitute waiver of IL Franchise Disclosure Act rights
IL §41 voids waivers of IL Act compliance (does not prevent settlements or FAA arbitration)
MD law may supersede FA on termination and renewal
Bankruptcy-triggered termination may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
May sue in MD for claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law; 3-year statute of limitations from grant
General Release (FA §16.8) does not release franchisor from MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law liability
No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment signed by franchisee waives claims under state franchise law including fraud in inducement
Prohibition on joining franchisee association is VOID
Requirement that franchisee assent to release/assignment/novation/waiver/estoppel depriving rights is VOID
Termination requires good cause (failure to comply and failure to cure after written notice with reasonable opportunity ≤30 days)

Non-renewal without fair compensation for FMV of inventory/supplies/equipment/fixtures/furnishings is VOID if term <5 years AND franchisee prohibited from continuing similar business in same area OR did not receive 6 months notice
Refusal to renew on terms generally available to other franchisees of same class is VOID (does not require a renewal provision)
Arbitration or litigation outside MI is VOID (except by post-dispute agreement)
Refusal to permit transfer except for good cause is VOID (does not prevent ROFR); good cause enumerated
Requirement to resell to franchisor items not uniquely identified with franchisor is VOID (ROFR permitted)
Franchisor's right to transfer obligations without ensuring fulfillment is VOID
MN law requires franchisor indemnification against third-party trademark infringement claims (subject to 10-day notice and tender of defense)
MN Stat §80C.14 subd 3-5: 90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure) and 180 days notice for non-renewal
General release does not relieve franchisor from MN Franchise Investment Law liability
MN Rule 2860.4400J prohibits requiring consent to liquidated damages
MN Stat §80C.21 and MN Rule 2860.4400J prohibit requiring out-of-state litigation, jury trial waiver, liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
Additional Item 3 disclosures required re: pending actions, convictions, and injunctive orders
Additional Item 4 bankruptcy disclosures for 10-year lookback on franchisor and Item 2 persons
Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
Franchisor will not assign except to assignee willing and able to assume obligations
NY law governs any cause of action arising under NY Franchises Law; NY General Business Law Article 33 §§680-695 may supersede inconsistent FA provisions
General release requirements on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law
Franchisor's injunctive relief only after proper proofs and authority grant
Liquidated damages provisions DELETED in entirety
ND law supersedes NY law and other FA provisions in conflict; FA governed by ND law
Provisions designating jurisdiction/venue outside ND are DELETED
Jury trial waiver DELETED
Release language does not relieve franchisor from ND Franchise Disclosure Act liability
Termination on bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
Release on transfer/renewal does not apply to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
Provisions restricting jurisdiction/venue outside RI or requiring non-RI governing law are VOID as to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
Per VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564, termination without reasonable cause is unlawful; FA default grounds that do not constitute 'reasonable cause' may not be enforceable
RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA on termination and renewal
Release/waiver does not include WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights except when in negotiated settlement with independent counsel
In conflict of laws, WA Franchise Investment Protection Act (Chapter 19.100 RCW) prevails
Transfer fees collectable only to extent of reasonable actual/estimated costs
Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed; franchisee may bring action in WA
Noncompete VOID against employees earning <$100K/yr (annualized) and independent contractors earning <$250K/yr (annualized) per RCW 49.62.020 and 49.62.030
RCW 49.62.060: franchisor cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring employees of same-franchisor franchisees or of the franchisor
